title: RV8GR ROM to DBUS to IBUS read path
purpose: Prove a ROM byte can drive DBUS and cross U7 onto IBUS during fetch/load, while ROM and U7 release safely during write or disabled states.

chips.role: DBUS to IBUS bridge during read; IBUS to DBUS bridge during write

behavior.rom_select: A15=0 selects ROM; A15=1 disables ROM.
behavior.rom_output: ROM /OE follows WR_DIR. Read has WR_DIR=0, store/write has WR_DIR=1.
behavior.rom_write: ROM /WE is held HIGH for ordinary CPU reads.
behavior.u7_read: Physical U7 A side is IBUS and B side is DBUS. With /OE=0 and DIR=0, standard 74HC245 behavior is DBUS_TO_IBUS: B drives A, so DBUS reaches IBUS.
behavior.bus_policy: Read phases allow exactly one active DBUS driver, ROM1, and one active IBUS bridge driver, U7; write/disabled phases force ROM output high-Z before another DBUS driver may take ownership.
behavior.write_safety: WR_DIR=1 disables ROM output before U7 is used as an IBUS to DBUS write bridge.

timing.read_window: T0/T1 fetch and T2 memory load require ROM selected, ROM /OE LOW, and U7 /OE LOW with DIR=0.
timing.deadband_boundary: ROM /OE high-Z time, U7 enable delay, DBUS R/C delay, and positive disable-to-enable deadband are required before treating this as a physical speed signoff.
unsafe_states: ROM output enabled while U7 writes IBUS to DBUS
U7 disabled while an instruction byte is expected on IBUS
A15=1 during a ROM fetch address

guarantees: Known ROM bytes cross from DBUS to IBUS through live AT28C256 and 74HC245 models
WR_DIR=1 disables ROM output and prevents ROM-vs-U7 write contention

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
